title: T5 Watchtower: Reconcile the two decision-queue concepts
prompt: Two mechanically unrelated systems share the name 'decision queue': the engine's mid-task HITL gate (SHIPPED in engine/orchestrator.py — a gated worker blocks on engine/store/needs-decision/<tid>.json, David answers via decisions/<tid>.json, the orchestrator resumes the live worker; wired but unexercised live, the live proof belongs to T1-01) and Watchtower's curation queue (DESIGN-ONLY — at-rest promote/reject over artifacts writing promotion records, never mutating canonical/; docs/watchtower/ plan+spec plus a 1-commit RVETS stub at apps/watchtower whose README tagline is literally 'a decision queue'). docs/comms-flow.md line 155 names this collision as gap #1 and docs/harness-evaluation.md row 5 already ruled the two mechanisms orthogonal and both needed. Produce ONE design note, docs/decision-queues.md, that locks the vocabulary ('HITL gate' = mid-task blocking mechanism; 'curation queue' = at-rest artifact promotion; 'decision queue'/'Needs-You' = the aggregate SURFACE only, never a mechanism or a store), pins both mechanisms' ground truth from disk evidence (never doc claims — comms-flow still says the gate lives only in the POC, which is stale), records the curation verb drift between plan.md and spec.md without resolving it (T5-05 owns that), states binding naming rules for the T5-01/T5-05/T5-06/T5-12 builds, and carries a docs-lag ledger for T8. Add one-line cross-link pins to docs/INDEX.md, docs/comms-flow.md section 7c, and docs/watchtower/plan.md; fix the apps/watchtower README tagline only if that repo is still the clean 1-commit stub. Change NO engine .py, NO watchtower client/server/shared code, NOTHING in canonical/ — this is a vocabulary artifact, not a build and not a merge. Read and execute the war game at backlog/wargames/T5-07-decision-queue-reconcile.md. Follow its moves in order; on any failure signal apply the counter-move; on any abort condition stop and write needs-decision/.
verify: docs/decision-queues.md exists with >=9 '## ' sections including the three-term vocabulary lock (grep hits for 'HITL gate', 'curation queue', and the surface-only ruling), both mechanisms' ground-truth inventories with file+line evidence, the recorded-not-resolved curation verb drift (grep 'Accept.*Rerun.*Promote.*Kill' hits), binding naming rules for the T5 builds, a docs-lag ledger, and follow-on one-liners. `grep -rn decision-queues.md docs/INDEX.md docs/comms-flow.md docs/watchtower/plan.md | wc -l` returns 3. A wg-t5-07 self-report exists in engine/store/results/ and the dark-factory commit is pushed. If Fork F1 Route A ran, ../watchtower has one new commit above 1f4d5e9 touching only README.md. Negative: no engine .py changed, canonical/ untouched, `git -C ../watchtower diff 1f4d5e9 --stat -- client server shared` empty, and engine/store/needs-decision/ has a wg-t5-07 entry only if an abort or the F1-B contradiction branch fired.
